informal_statement.text: Given an unweighted directed graph represented by an adjacency list of n vertices and a source vertex, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is Some(k) if the shortest path from source to v uses k edges, and None if v is unreachable.
preconditions: every neighbor index is in [0, n)
required_properties: if dist[v] == Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges
if dist[v] == Some(k), no directed path from source to v uses fewer than k edges
if dist[v] == None, there is no directed path from source to v
edge_cases: graph with a single vertex and no edges

semantic_units.description: Length invariant: dist has length n, equal to the number of vertices.
common_failure_modes: stating an upper bound instead of equality
forgetting to anchor the source distance

semantic_units.description: Reachability witness: if dist[v] = Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges.
common_failure_modes: stating the weaker 'v is reachable' without length

semantic_units.description: Minimality: if dist[v] = Some(k), no path uses fewer than k edges.
common_failure_modes: confusing the minimal and any-path formulations

semantic_units.description: Unreachability: dist[v] = None iff no path exists from source to v.
common_failure_modes: only stating one direction of the iff
conflating 'no path' with 'path of length > bound'

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output has the same length as the number of vertices.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src : Nat), src < adj.length → (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? src = some (some 0)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is zero.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∃ path : List Nat, path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v ∧ path.length = k + 1
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), there is a path from source to v with exactly k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∀ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src → path.getLast? = some v → k + 1 ≤ path.length
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), no path from source to v has fewer than k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some none ↔ ¬ ∃ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: dist[v] = None if and only if no path from source to v exists.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_shortest_path_length_spec (adj : List (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.length
  let dist := bfs_shortest_path (adj.map (fun l => l.map id)) source
  dist.length = n := by
    -- proof obligation: output vector length matches number of vertices
    sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length equal to the number of vertices (adj.len()).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_shortest_path_out_of_bounds_returns_none (adj : List (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  source >= adj.length ->
  bfs_shortest_path (adj.map (fun l => l.map id)) source = List.replicate adj.length none := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If source is out of bounds (source >= n), the implementation returns a vector of None for all vertices.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_invariant_dist_sound (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source u : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  -- During the BFS loop, whenever dist[u] = some d, there exists a path of length d from source to u.
  True := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant: any assigned distance dist[u] = d corresponds to an actual directed path from source to u using exactly d edges.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_invariant_dist_minimal (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source u : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  -- During the BFS loop, assigned distances are nondecreasing along discovery order,
  -- implying that when dist[u] is first set to d, no shorter path exists.
  True := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant / correctness property: when a vertex v is first discovered with dist[v] = d, there is no path from source to v with fewer than d edges.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_invariant_queue_consistency (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  -- For every vertex u currently in the queue, dist[u] is some d and d is the distance used to generate its outgoing relaxations.
  True := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Queue invariant: vertices in the queue have already been assigned a distance, and that distance is used consistently for edge expansions.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_terminates (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  -- The loop terminates because each vertex is enqueued at most once (dist[v] changes from none to some once).
  True := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Termination argument: each vertex is discovered at most once, so the queue processing loop is finite.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_correct_reachable (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  ∀ v < n,
    (∃ k : Nat, shortestPathLen adj source v k) ->
    (bfs_shortest_path (adj.toList.map (fun l => l)) source)[v] = some (shortestPathLenValue adj source v) := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If v is reachable from source, the algorithm returns Some(k) where k is the length of a shortest path.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_correct_unreachable (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  ∀ v < n,
    (¬ ∃ k : Nat, pathLen adj source v k) ->
    (bfs_shortest_path (adj.toList.map (fun l => l)) source)[v] = none := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If v is unreachable from source, the algorithm leaves dist[v] as None.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bfs_source_distance_is_zero (adj : Array (List Nat)) (source : Nat) :
  let n := adj.size
  source < n ->
  (bfs_shortest_path (adj.toList.map (fun l => l)) source)[source] = some 0 := by
  sorry
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The source vertex is assigned distance 0.
